steps.title: Set equations equal and solve for x
steps.content:
Since both expressions equal $y$, set them equal:
$$-2x - 10 = 2x + 16$$
Combine like terms by moving all $x$ terms to one side:
$$-2x - 2x = 16 + 10$$
$$-4x = 26$$
$$x = -\frac{13}{2} = -6.5$$

steps.title: Substitute to find y
steps.content:
Use either original equation. Substituting $x = -6.5$ into $y = 2x + 16$:
$$y = 2(-6.5) + 16 = -13 + 16 = 3$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
